name: NONOSSIFYING FIBROMA
definition: FIBROXANTHOMA = NONOSTEOGENIC FIBROMA = XANTHOMA = nonossifying fibroma with extraskeletal manifestations in children

demographics.Frequency: up to 40% of all children > 2 years of age
demographics.Etiology: lesion resulting from proliferative activity of a fibrous cortical defect that has
demographics.Histo: interlacing whorled bundles of spindle-shaped fibroblasts + scattered multinucleated

dominant_finding: 20% hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI ← massive aggregation of foamy
discriminating_features: well-circumscribed multiloculated bubbly oval osteolytic area
alignment along long axis of bone, > 2 cm in length
scalloped sclerotic margin toward medulla; V- or U-shaped at one end
mild expansile remodeling = endosteal scalloping + thinning ± overlying bulge

general: well-circumscribed multiloculated bubbly oval osteolytic area
alignment along long axis of bone, > 2 cm in length
scalloped sclerotic margin toward medulla; V- or U-shaped at one end
mild expansile remodeling = endosteal scalloping + thinning ± overlying bulge
NUC: minimal / mild uptake on bone scan
MR: 80% hypointense on T1WI + T2WI ← extensive hypocellular fibrous tissue, hemosiderin
20% hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI ← massive aggregation of foamy
peripheral hypointense rim + internal septation ← marginal reactive sclerosis +
intense contrast enhancement (in 80%) / marginal septal enhancement (in 20%) on T1WI
